{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-kami}}・{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (East Main Hall) | 創建=Era of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|kensha}} | 例祭=Namura Festival (April 20)
Shikinen Festival (every 33 years, September 5) | 神事=Sekku Festival (May 5) | 本殿=Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} (West Main Hall)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ryuo|Q1087560}}, {{jalink|Gamo District|Q931658}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. It is a high-ranking {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine rank system}}, it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, it was listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{jalink|Anna|Q1373003}} 2 ({{jalink|969|Q23708}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{jalink|Kinpusen}} in {{jalink|Yoshino|Q15410122}} of {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{jalink|Kannin|Q1193951}} 1 ({{jalink|1017|Q19249}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{jalink|kadomatsu|Q2667233}}) to the {{jalink|Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|court}}, it became an annual custom; then {{jalink|Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{jalink|Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} 5 ({{jalink|1536|Q6457}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{jalink|Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{jalink|Nakanomikado Munefuji}} and {{jalink|Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} era ({{jalink|1573|Q6678}}-{{jalink|1593|Q6772}}), {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{jalink|1920|Q2155}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} 9) it was promoted to {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{jalink|Namurahiko-no-kami}} (なむらひこがみ), {{jalink|Namurahime-no-kami}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{jalink|Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-kami}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{jalink|National Treasure|Q1139795}}) - rebuilt in {{jalink|Tokuji|Q1327170}} 3 ({{jalink|1308|Q5723}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{jalink|Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|Jūzenji Shrine}} ({{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. * {{jalink|Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{jalink|Ebisu|Q1129330|Ebisu Shrine}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{jalink|Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|Gokoku Shrine}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{jalink|Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} 5 ({{jalink|1536|Q6457}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{jalink|Daiei|Q1193564}} 2 ({{jalink|1522|Q6299}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{jalink|Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|Sasaki Shrine}} * {{jalink|Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{jalink|kofun|Q1141225}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{jalink|Fudō Myōō|Q337624}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{jalink|miyaza|Q11454763}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{jalink|Yabusame|Q1886157|ryūsenma}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{jalink|Keicho|Q1328185}} 4 ({{jalink|1599|Q6803}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{jalink|Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{jalink|Biwako Line|Q804135}}: get off at {{jalink|Omihachiman Station|Q1042782}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{jalink|Meishin Expressway|Q904481|Meishin}} {{jalink|Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|Ryuo IC}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815}} * {{jalink|List of National Treasures of Japan}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なむらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の国宝]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]]

Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{jalink|Yabusame|Q1886157|ryūsenma}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{jalink|Keicho|Q1328185}} 4 ({{jalink|1599|Q6803}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{jalink|Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{jalink|Biwako Line|Q804135}}: get off at {{jalink|Omihachiman Station|Q1042782}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{jalink|Meishin Expressway|Q904481|Meishin}} {{jalink|Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|Ryuo IC}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815}} * {{jalink|List of National Treasures of Japan}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なむらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の国宝]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]]

{{commons category|Category:Namura Jinja}} [[EN:Namura Shrine]] [[JA:苗村神社]] [[MS:Kuil Namura]] {{wikidata link|Q11616123}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Namura Shrine | 画像=[[画像:Shiga_Namura1.jpg|270px]]
West Main Hall (National Treasure)
[[画像:Shiga_Namura2.jpg|270px]]
East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) | 所在地={{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Gamo District|Q931658}}{{jalink|Ryuo|Q1087560}} Ayado 467 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|53.5|N|136|7|40.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Namura Shrine}} | 祭神={{jalink|Namurahiko-no-kami}}、{{jalink|Namurahime-no-kami}}、{{jalink|Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692}} (West Main Hall)
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-kami}}・{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (East Main Hall) | 創建=Era of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|kensha}} | 例祭=Namura Festival (April 20)
Shikinen Festival (every 33 years, September 5) | 神事=Sekku Festival (May 5) | 本殿=Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} (West Main Hall)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Ryuo|Q1087560}}, {{jalink|Gamo District|Q931658}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. It is a high-ranking {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine rank system}}, it was a {{jalink|kensha}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, it was listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{jalink|Anna|Q1373003}} 2 ({{jalink|969|Q23708}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{jalink|Kinpusen}} in {{jalink|Yoshino|Q15410122}} of {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{jalink|Kannin|Q1193951}} 1 ({{jalink|1017|Q19249}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{jalink|kadomatsu|Q2667233}}) to the {{jalink|Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|court}}, it became an annual custom; then {{jalink|Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{jalink|Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} 5 ({{jalink|1536|Q6457}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{jalink|Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{jalink|Nakanomikado Munefuji}} and {{jalink|Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} era ({{jalink|1573|Q6678}}-{{jalink|1593|Q6772}}), {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{jalink|1881|Q7808}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{jalink|1920|Q2155}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} 9) it was promoted to {{jalink|kensha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{jalink|Namurahiko-no-kami}} (なむらひこがみ), {{jalink|Namurahime-no-kami}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{jalink|Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-kami}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{jalink|National Treasure|Q1139795}}) - rebuilt in {{jalink|Tokuji|Q1327170}} 3 ({{jalink|1308|Q5723}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{jalink|Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|Jūzenji Shrine}} ({{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. * {{jalink|Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{jalink|Ebisu|Q1129330|Ebisu Shrine}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{jalink|Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|Gokoku Shrine}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{jalink|Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} 5 ({{jalink|1536|Q6457}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{jalink|Daiei|Q1193564}} 2 ({{jalink|1522|Q6299}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{jalink|Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|Sasaki Shrine}} * {{jalink|Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{jalink|kofun|Q1141225}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{jalink|Fudō Myōō|Q337624}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{jalink|miyaza|Q11454763}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{jalink|Yabusame|Q1886157|ryūsenma}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{jalink|Keicho|Q1328185}} 4 ({{jalink|1599|Q6803}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{jalink|Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{jalink|Biwako Line|Q804135}}: get off at {{jalink|Omihachiman Station|Q1042782}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{jalink|Meishin Expressway|Q904481|Meishin}} {{jalink|Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|Ryuo IC}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815}} * {{jalink|List of National Treasures of Japan}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なむらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の国宝]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

画像=[[画像:Shiga_Namura1.jpg|270px]]
West Main Hall (National Treasure)
[[画像:Shiga_Namura2.jpg|270px]]
East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658}}{{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560}} Ayado 467 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|53.5|N|136|7|40.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Namura Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill||lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|UNKNOWN}}、{{ill||lt=Namurahime-no-kami|UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} (West Main Hall)
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami}}・{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (East Main Hall) | 創建=Era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} | 例祭=Namura Festival (April 20)
Shikinen Festival (every 33 years, September 5) | 神事=Sekku Festival (May 5) | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} (West Main Hall)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560}}, {{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a high-ranking {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank system}}, it was a {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, it was listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|WD=Q1373003}} 2 ({{ill|lt=969|Q23708|WD=Q23708}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{ill||lt=Kinpusen|UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q15410122|WD=Q15410122}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill||lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill||lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill||lt=Namurahime-no-kami|UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1308|Q5723|WD=Q5723}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815}} * {{ill||lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なむらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の国宝]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

On March 28, {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|WD=Q1373003}} 2 ({{ill|lt=969|Q23708|WD=Q23708}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinpusen|UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q15410122|WD=Q15410122}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1308|Q5723|WD=Q5723}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なむらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の国宝]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560}}, {{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a high-ranking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank system}}, it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, it was listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|WD=Q1373003}} 2 ({{ill|lt=969|Q23708|WD=Q23708}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinpusen|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q15410122|WD=Q15410122}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なむらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の国宝]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[画像:Shiga_Namura2.jpg|270px]]
East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658}}{{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560}} Ayado 467 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|53.5|N|136|7|40.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Namura Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} (West Main Hall)
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami}}・{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (East Main Hall) | 創建=Era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} | 例祭=Namura Festival (April 20)
Shikinen Festival (every 33 years, September 5) | 神事=Sekku Festival (May 5) | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} (West Main Hall)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560}}, {{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a high-ranking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank system}}, it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, it was listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|WD=Q1373003}} 2 ({{ill|lt=969|Q23708|WD=Q23708}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinpusen|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q15410122|WD=Q15410122}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1308|Q5723|WD=Q5723}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なむらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の国宝]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

画像=[[画像:Shiga_Namura1.jpg|270px]]
West Main Hall (National Treasure)
[[画像:Shiga_Namura2.jpg|270px]]
East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658}}{{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560}} Ayado 467 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|53.5|N|136|7|40.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Namura Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} (West Main Hall)
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami}}・{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (East Main Hall) | 創建=Era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭=Namura Festival (April 20)
Shikinen Festival (every 33 years, September 5) | 神事=Sekku Festival (May 5) | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} (West Main Hall)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560}}, {{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a high-ranking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank system}}, it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, it was listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|WD=Q1373003}} 2 ({{ill|lt=969|Q23708|WD=Q23708}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinpusen|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q15410122|WD=Q15410122}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1308|Q5723|WD=Q5723}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なむらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の国宝]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

West Main Hall (National Treasure)
[[画像:Shiga_Namura2.jpg|270px]]
East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658}}{{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560}} Ayado 467 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|53.5|N|136|7|40.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Namura Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} (West Main Hall)
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami}}・{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (East Main Hall) | 創建=Era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭=Namura Festival (April 20)
Shikinen Festival (every 33 years, September 5) | 神事=Sekku Festival (May 5) | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} (West Main Hall)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560}}, {{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a high-ranking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank system}}, it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, it was listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|WD=Q1373003}} 2 ({{ill|lt=969|Q23708|WD=Q23708}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinpusen|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q15410122|WD=Q15410122}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1308|Q5723|WD=Q5723}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なむらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の国宝]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
