Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Namura Shrine | 画像=[[画像:Shiga_Namura1.jpg|270px]]
West Main Hall (National Treasure)
[[画像:Shiga_Namura2.jpg|270px]]
East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658|1=Gamō District, Shiga}}{{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560|1=Ryūō, Shiga}} Ayado 467 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|53.5|N|136|7|40.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Namura Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (West Main Hall)
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}・{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (East Main Hall) | 創建=Era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭=Namura Festival (April 20)
Shikinen Festival (every 33 years, September 5) | 神事=Sekku Festival (May 5) | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (West Main Hall)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560|1=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658|1=Gamō District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a high-ranking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it was listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|WD=Q1373003|1=Anna (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=969|Q23708|WD=Q23708|1=969}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinpusen|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q15410122|WD=Q15410122|1=Yoshino}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951|1=Kannin}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249|1=1017}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233|1=Kadomatsu}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court|1=Imperial Court of Japan}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467|1=Emperor Go-Nara}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822|1=Yamashina Tokitsugu}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155|1=1920}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1308|Q5723|WD=Q5723|1=1308}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine|1=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327|1=Daijingū}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624|1=Acala}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763|1=miyaza}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma|1=Yabusame}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803|1=1599}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678|1=Chunichi Shimbun}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135|1=Biwako Line}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782|1=Ōmi-Hachiman Station}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin|1=Meishin Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC|1=Ryuo interchange}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Buildings)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]][[Category:qq]]

Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Namura Shrine | 画像=[[画像:Shiga_Namura1.jpg|270px]]
West Main Hall (National Treasure)
[[画像:Shiga_Namura2.jpg|270px]]
East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658|1=Gamō District, Shiga}}{{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560|1=Ryūō, Shiga}} Ayado 467 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|53.5|N|136|7|40.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Namura Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (West Main Hall)
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}・{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (East Main Hall) | 創建=Era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭=Namura Festival (April 20)
Shikinen Festival (every 33 years, September 5) | 神事=Sekku Festival (May 5) | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (West Main Hall)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560|1=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658|1=Gamō District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a high-ranking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it was listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|WD=Q1373003|1=Anna (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=969|Q23708|WD=Q23708|1=969}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinpusen|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q15410122|WD=Q15410122|1=Yoshino}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951|1=Kannin}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249|1=1017}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233|1=Kadomatsu}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court|1=Imperial Court of Japan}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467|1=Emperor Go-Nara}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822|1=Yamashina Tokitsugu}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155|1=1920}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1308|Q5723|WD=Q5723|1=1308}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine|1=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327|1=Daijingū}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624|1=Acala}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763|1=miyaza}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma|1=Yabusame}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803|1=1599}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678|1=Chunichi Shimbun}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135|1=Biwako Line}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782|1=Ōmi-Hachiman Station}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin|1=Meishin Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC|1=Ryuo interchange}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Buildings)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]][[Category:qq]]

East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658|1=Gamō District, Shiga}}{{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560|1=Ryūō, Shiga}} Ayado 467 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|53.5|N|136|7|40.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Namura Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (West Main Hall)
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}・{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (East Main Hall) | 創建=Era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭=Namura Festival (April 20)
Shikinen Festival (every 33 years, September 5) | 神事=Sekku Festival (May 5) | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (West Main Hall)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560|1=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658|1=Gamō District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a high-ranking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it was listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|WD=Q1373003|1=Anna (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=969|Q23708|WD=Q23708|1=969}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinpusen|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q15410122|WD=Q15410122|1=Yoshino}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951|1=Kannin}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249|1=1017}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233|1=Kadomatsu}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court|1=Imperial Court of Japan}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467|1=Emperor Go-Nara}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822|1=Yamashina Tokitsugu}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155|1=1920}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1308|Q5723|WD=Q5723|1=1308}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine|1=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327|1=Daijingū}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624|1=Acala}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763|1=miyaza}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma|1=Yabusame}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803|1=1599}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678|1=Chunichi Shimbun}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135|1=Biwako Line}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782|1=Ōmi-Hachiman Station}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin|1=Meishin Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC|1=Ryuo interchange}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Buildings)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]][[Category:qq]]

One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560|1=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658|1=Gamō District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a high-ranking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it was listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|WD=Q1373003|1=Anna (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=969|Q23708|WD=Q23708|1=969}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinpusen|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q15410122|WD=Q15410122|1=Yoshino}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951|1=Kannin}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249|1=1017}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233|1=Kadomatsu}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court|1=Imperial Court of Japan}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467|1=Emperor Go-Nara}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822|1=Yamashina Tokitsugu}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155|1=1920}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1308|Q5723|WD=Q5723|1=1308}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine|1=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327|1=Daijingū}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624|1=Acala}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763|1=miyaza}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma|1=Yabusame}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803|1=1599}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678|1=Chunichi Shimbun}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135|1=Biwako Line}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782|1=Ōmi-Hachiman Station}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin|1=Meishin Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC|1=Ryuo interchange}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Buildings)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]][[Category:qq]]

In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951|1=Kannin}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249|1=1017}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233|1=Kadomatsu}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court|1=Imperial Court of Japan}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467|1=Emperor Go-Nara}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822|1=Yamashina Tokitsugu}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155|1=1920}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1308|Q5723|WD=Q5723|1=1308}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine|1=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327|1=Daijingū}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624|1=Acala}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763|1=miyaza}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma|1=Yabusame}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803|1=1599}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678|1=Chunichi Shimbun}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135|1=Biwako Line}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782|1=Ōmi-Hachiman Station}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin|1=Meishin Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC|1=Ryuo interchange}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Buildings)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]]

Shikinen Festival (every 33 years, September 5) | 神事=Sekku Festival (May 5) | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (West Main Hall)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560|1=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658|1=Gamō District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a high-ranking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it was listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|WD=Q1373003|1=Anna (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=969|Q23708|WD=Q23708|1=969}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinpusen|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q15410122|WD=Q15410122|1=Yoshino}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951|1=Kannin}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249|1=1017}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233|1=Kadomatsu}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court|1=Imperial Court of Japan}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467|1=Emperor Go-Nara}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822|1=Yamashina Tokitsugu}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155|1=1920}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1308|Q5723|WD=Q5723|1=1308}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine|1=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327|1=Daijingū}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624|1=Acala}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763|1=miyaza}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma|1=Yabusame}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803|1=1599}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678|1=Chunichi Shimbun}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135|1=Biwako Line}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782|1=Ōmi-Hachiman Station}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin|1=Meishin Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC|1=Ryuo interchange}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Buildings)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]]

Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Namura Shrine | 画像=[[画像:Shiga_Namura1.jpg|270px]]
West Main Hall (National Treasure)
[[画像:Shiga_Namura2.jpg|270px]]
East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658|1=Gamō District, Shiga}}{{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560|1=Ryūō, Shiga}} Ayado 467 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|53.5|N|136|7|40.5|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Namura Shrine}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (West Main Hall)
{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}・{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (East Main Hall) | 創建=Era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 例祭=Namura Festival (April 20)
Shikinen Festival (every 33 years, September 5) | 神事=Sekku Festival (May 5) | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (West Main Hall)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (East Main Hall) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Namura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Namura Shrine''' (なむらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ryuo|Q1087560|WD=Q1087560|1=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Gamo District|Q931658|WD=Q931658|1=Gamō District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a high-ranking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as Nagamura (Namura) Shrine. Under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is the head shrine for parishioners across 33 nearby villages. === History === Its detailed foundation is unknown, but shrine tradition says it began as an ancestral-spirit cult in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. At the site of the Higashi-Namura Kofun group, a shrine called Nagamura ("naga" as highest rank and "mura" as an old character for village), phonetically matching Namura, was established; this is the present East Main Hall. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it was listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and ranked as a Shikinaisha. On March 28, {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|WD=Q1373003|1=Anna (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=969|Q23708|WD=Q23708|1=969}}), the divine spirit of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kinpusen|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q15410122|WD=Q15410122|1=Yoshino}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} was enshrined west of the sacred precinct, and a new shrine building was constructed. This shrine came to be called the West Main Hall in contrast to the previous main hall (East Main Hall). In the first month of {{ill|lt=Kannin|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951|1=Kannin}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249|1=1017}}), after presenting young pine trees for the imperial New Year gate decorations ({{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|WD=Q2667233|1=Kadomatsu}}) to the {{ill|lt=Imperial court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=court|1=Imperial Court of Japan}}, it became an annual custom; then {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijo|Q374087|WD=Q374087|1=Emperor Go-Ichijō}} bestowed the title Namura, and it came to be called Namura. On March 2, {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}), it received the divine rank of "Senior First Rank" from {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|Q349467|WD=Q349467|1=Emperor Go-Nara}}. On April 9 of the same year, envoy {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanomikado Munefuji|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Yamashina Tokitsugu|Q11470822|WD=Q11470822|1=Yamashina Tokitsugu}} dedicated the written record of divine rank. On May 9, the imperial plaque "Senior First Rank Namura Daimyojin" was also bestowed. During the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1593|Q6772|WD=Q6772|1=1593}}), {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} donated a full horse harness and seven swords. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 14) it became a gosha, and in {{ill|lt=1920|Q2155|WD=Q2155|1=1920}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 9) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahiko-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひこがみ), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Namurahime-no-kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (なむらひめがみ), and {{ill|lt=Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} (くにのさづちのみこと); deities of the West Main Hall. * Deities in the shared hall - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (おおくにぬしのみこと), {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (ことしろぬしのかみ), and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと); deities of the East Main Hall. === Precinct === === West precinct === * West Main Hall ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|WD=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1308|Q5723|WD=Q5723|1=1308}}); three-bay nagare-zukuri, cypress-bark roof. * Heiden * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|Q656451|WD=Q656451|lt=Jūzenji Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in mid-{{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. * {{ill|lt=Hachiman-gū|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu Shrine|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} * Ayano Shrine * Chūmon gate * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} * Shinko storehouse (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1536|Q6457|WD=Q6457|1=1536}}). * Statue of Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto as a child guardian * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Storage building * Ryujin Shrine * Assembly hall * Fudō Hall - formerly the Namura-gu palace-guarding goma hall. * Rōmon gate (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1522|Q6299|WD=Q6299|1=1522}}). * Horse grounds === East precinct === * East Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - rebuilt in mid-Muromachi period. * Tenjin Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sasaki Shrine|Q11553074|WD=Q11553074|lt=Sasaki Shrine|1=Sasaki Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q109420327|WD=Q109420327|1=Daijingū}} * Higashi-Namura Kofun group - group of {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} from the latter half of the 6th century. === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * West Main Hall, plus: one zushi shrine cabinet and one munafuda board === Important Cultural Properties === * East Main Hall, plus: two munafuda boards * Main hall of Hachiman Shrine * Main hall of Jūzenji Shrine * Rōmon gate, plus: two old timbers * Shinko storehouse * Wooden standing statue of {{ill|lt=Fudō Myōō|Q337624|WD=Q337624|1=Acala}} === Festivals === ;Namura Festival An ancient grand festival held every year on April 20. With a sacred horse procession from nine {{ill|lt=miyaza|Q11454763|WD=Q11454763|1=miyaza}} seats, after dedication rites ten sacred horses and three portable shrines proceed in line and make a procession to the resting place. ;Sekku Festival A grand festival on May 5 celebrating children's growth. Child mikoshi dedication and ten sacred horses run along the approach in traditional costume in the dynamic "{{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|WD=Q1886157|lt=ryūsenma|1=Yabusame}}" event, attracting many visitors all day. ;Shikinen Festival (33-year grand festival) {{要出典範囲|date=2017年5月|Formerly, a grand festival with participation from 33 parish villages was held every year on September 5.}} Since {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1599|Q6803|WD=Q6803|1=1599}}), it has been held once every 33 years{{Cite news ja | title = Once every 33 years: Ryuo, Namura Shrine Grand Festival; prefectural university survey team publishes record book | newspaper = {{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678|1=Chunichi Shimbun}} | date = 2016-03-23 | author = Kanako Kawabe | publisher = Chunichi Shimbun Co. | edition= morning, Biwako edition |page=24 }}. The 2014 event (Heisei 26) is said to have been the 14th. === Blessings === * Matchmaking * Prosperous business / success and good fortune, abundant harvests * Safe childbirth and children * Protection from misfortune === Address === 520-2524, 467 Ayado, Ryuo-cho, Gamo-gun, Shiga Prefecture === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135|1=Biwako Line}}: get off at {{ill|lt=Omihachiman Station|Q1042782|WD=Q1042782|1=Ōmi-Hachiman Station}}, 20 min by bus, get off at Kawamori bus stop * {{ill|lt=Meishin Expressway|Q904481|WD=Q904481|lt=Meishin|1=Meishin Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Ryuo Interchange|Q11180595|WD=Q11180595|lt=Ryuo IC|1=Ryuo interchange}}: 10 min by car ;Parking 20 regular cars, 5 large buses === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shinto shrines|Q2517815|WD=Q2517815|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan|1=UNKNOWN}} === External links=== * [https://namurajinjya.ryuoh.org/ Official homepage of Namura Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Namura Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Buildings)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:竜王町の建築物]] [[Category:竜王町の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
